title: Detecting Connected Components in a Graph

statement.markdown: Given a graph represented as an adjacency list, write a function to detect the number of connected components in the graph. The function should return the number of connected components. You can assume that the graph is undirected and unweighted.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be provided in the following format: [ num_nodes ] [ node1 edge1 node1 edge2 ... node2 edge1 node2 edge2 ... ]
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a single integer representing the number of connected components in the graph.
statement.notes: This problem does not require you to find the connected components themselves, only the number of connected components.

examples.explanation: The graph has two connected components: [1,2,3] and [4]

hints.text: Use DFS or BFS to traverse the graph
Count the number of connected components as you traverse the graph
